name: cymothoa
title: cymothoa

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=37ce47c4744d8f170cf42e82bdaff83b05ecc528, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

commands.name: cymothoa

arguments.description: memory region name for shellcode injection (default /lib/ld)
arguments.raw: -l	memory region name for shellcode injection (default /lib/ld)

arguments.description: memory region name for persistent memory (default /lib/ld)
arguments.raw: -m	memory region name for persistent memory (default /lib/ld)

arguments.description: create payload thread (probably you need also -F)
arguments.raw: -b	create payload thread (probably you need also -F)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# cymothoa -h
                             _                 
                         _  | |                
 ____ _   _ ____   ___ _| |_| |__   ___  _____ 
/ ___) | | |    \ / _ (_   _)  _ \ / _ \(____ |
 ( (___| |_| | | | | |_| || |_| | | | |_| / ___ |
\____)\__  |_|_|_|\___/  \__)_| |_|\___/\_____|
     (____/  
 Ver.1 (beta) - Runtime shellcode injection, for stealthy backdoors...
 
 By codwizard (codwizard@gmail.com) and crossbower (crossbower@gmail.com)
 from ES-Malaria by ElectronicSouls (http://www.0x4553.org).
 
 Usage:
 	cymothoa -p <pid> -s <shellcode_number> [options]
 
 Main options:
 	-p	process pid
 	-s	shellcode number
 	-l	memory region name for shellcode injection (default /lib/ld)
 	  	search for "r-xp" permissions, see /proc/pid/maps...
 	-m	memory region name for persistent memory (default /lib/ld)
 	  	search for "rw-p" permissions, see /proc/pid/maps...
 	-h	print this help screen
 	-S	list available shellcodes
 
 Injection options (overwrite payload flags):
 	-f	fork parent process
 	-F	don't fork parent process
 	-b	create payload thread (probably you need also -F)
 	-B	don't create payload thread
 	-w	pass persistent memory address
 	-W	don't pass persistent memory address
 	-a	use alarm scheduler
 	-A	don't use alarm scheduler
 	-t	use setitimer scheduler
 	-T	don't use setitimer scheduler
 
 Payload arguments:
 	-j	set timer (seconds)
 	-k	set timer (microseconds)
 	-x	set the IP
 	-y	set the port number
 	-r	set the port number 2
 	-z	set the username (4 bytes)
 	-o	set the password (8 bytes)
 	-c	set the script code (ex: "#!/bin/sh\nls; exit 0")
 	  	escape codes will not be interpreted...

local_help.--help: Error with option: -. Check the usage...

apt_metadata.description:
Stealth backdooring tool
Cymothoa is a stealth backdooring tool, that inject
backdoor's shellcode into an existing process. The tool
uses the ptrace library (available on nearly all * nix), to
manipulate processes and infect them.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bgrep because ### cymothoa.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bgrep with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bgrep --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### cymothoa.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install cymothoa
Install with: sudo apt install cymothoa
